questions.question: Which of the following logic gates produces a HIGH output only when all of its inputs are HIGH?
options: OR gate
XOR gate
NAND gate
NOR gate
AND gate

questions.question: In a CPU, what is the primary function of the Program Counter (PC)?
options: Stores the result of the last arithmetic operation.
Holds the instruction currently being executed.
Points to the memory address of the next instruction to be fetched.
Manages the data flow between registers.
Acts as a temporary storage for function return addresses.

questions.question: In the digital IC design flow, what is the primary purpose of 'Logic Equivalence Checking (LEC)'?
options: To verify the functional correctness of a design against its specification using test vectors.
To ensure that the post-synthesis netlist is logically identical to the pre-synthesis RTL design.
To detect and correct timing violations in the clock paths.
To optimize the physical placement of standard cells for minimal area and power.
To simulate power consumption across different operating modes.

questions.question: Which of the following factors is a primary reason for the increasing complexity and cost of manufacturing at advanced semiconductor technology nodes (e.g., 7nm, 5nm)?
options: Decreased demand for integrated circuits.
Reduced need for specialized cleanroom environments.
The increasing difficulty and cost associated with advanced lithography techniques and defect control.
Shift from silicon to germanium as the primary substrate material.
Simplification of device physics at smaller dimensions.

questions.question: In computer systems employing virtual memory, what is the primary function of the Memory Management Unit (MMU)?
options: To control the flow of data between the CPU and I/O devices.
To store frequently accessed instructions for faster retrieval.
To translate virtual addresses generated by the CPU into physical addresses in main memory.
To manage the allocation and deallocation of processor registers.
To synchronize access to shared memory resources in multi-core systems.
